speaker: CAPULET
text: But Montague is bound as well as I,

speaker: CAPULET
text: In penalty alike, and ’tis not hard, I think,

speaker: CAPULET
text: For men so old as we to keep the peace.

speaker: PARIS
text: Of honorable reckoning are you both,

speaker: PARIS
text: And pity ’tis you lived at odds so long.

speaker: PARIS
text: But now, my lord, what say you to my suit?

speaker: CAPULET
text: But saying o’er what I have said before.

speaker: CAPULET
text: My child is yet a stranger in the world.

speaker: CAPULET
text: She hath not seen the change of fourteen years.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Let two more summers wither in their pride

speaker: CAPULET
text: Ere we may think her ripe to be a bride.

speaker: PARIS
text: Younger than she are happy mothers made.

speaker: CAPULET
text: And too soon marred are those so early made.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Earth hath swallowed all my hopes but she;

speaker: CAPULET
text: She’s the hopeful lady of my earth.

speaker: CAPULET
text: But woo her, gentle Paris, get her heart;

speaker: CAPULET
text: My will to her consent is but a part.

speaker: CAPULET
text: And, she agreed, within her scope of choice

speaker: CAPULET
text: Lies my consent and fair according voice.

speaker: CAPULET
text: This night I hold an old accustomed feast,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Whereto I have invited many a guest

speaker: CAPULET
text: Such as I love; and you among the store,

speaker: CAPULET
text: One more, most welcome, makes my number more.

speaker: CAPULET
text: At my poor house look to behold this night

speaker: CAPULET
text: Earth-treading stars that make dark heaven light.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Such comfort as do lusty young men feel

speaker: CAPULET
text: When well-appareled April on the heel

speaker: CAPULET
text: Of limping winter treads, even such delight

speaker: CAPULET
text: Among fresh fennel buds shall you this night

speaker: CAPULET
text: Inherit at my house. Hear all, all see,

speaker: CAPULET
text: And like her most whose merit most shall be;

speaker: CAPULET
text: Which, on more view of many, mine, being one,

speaker: CAPULET
text: May stand in number, though in reck’ning none.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Come go with me.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Go, sirrah, trudge about

speaker: CAPULET
text: Through fair Verona, find those persons out

speaker: CAPULET
text: Whose names are written there, and to them say

speaker: CAPULET
text: My house and welcome on their pleasure stay.

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: Find them out whose names are written

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: here! It is written that the shoemaker should

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: meddle with his yard and the tailor with his last, the

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: fisher with his pencil and the painter with his nets.

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: But I am sent to find those persons whose names

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: are here writ, and can never find what names the

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: writing person hath here writ. I must to the learned.

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: In good time!

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Tut, man, one fire burns out another’s burning;

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: One pain is lessened by another’s anguish.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Turn giddy, and be helped by backward turning.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: One desperate grief cures with another’s languish.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Take thou some new infection to thy eye,

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: And the rank poison of the old will die.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Your plantain leaf is excellent for that.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: For what, I pray thee?

speaker: ROMEO
text: For your broken shin.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Why Romeo, art thou mad?

speaker: ROMEO
text: Not mad, but bound more than a madman is,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Shut up in prison, kept without my food,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Whipped and tormented, and—good e’en, good fellow.

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: God gi’ good e’en. I pray, sir, can you

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: read?

speaker: ROMEO
text: Ay, mine own fortune in my misery.

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: Perhaps you have learned it without

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: book. But I pray, can you read anything you see?

speaker: ROMEO
text: Ay, if I know the letters and the language.

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: You say honestly. Rest you merry.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Stay, fellow. I can read.

speaker: ROMEO
text: A fair assembly. Whither should they come?

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: Up.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Whither? To supper?

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: To our house.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Whose house?

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: My master’s.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Indeed I should have asked thee that before.

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: Now I’ll tell you without asking. My

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: master is the great rich Capulet, and, if you be not

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: of the house of Montagues, I pray come and crush a

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: cup of wine. Rest you merry.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: At this same ancient feast of Capulet’s

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Sups the fair Rosaline whom thou so loves,

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: With all the admirèd beauties of Verona.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Go thither, and with unattainted eye

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Compare her face with some that I shall show,

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: And I will make thee think thy swan a crow.

speaker: ROMEO
text: When the devout religion of mine eye

speaker: ROMEO
text: Maintains such falsehood, then turn tears to fire;

speaker: ROMEO
text: And these who, often drowned, could never die,

speaker: ROMEO
text: Transparent heretics, be burnt for liars.

speaker: ROMEO
text: One fairer than my love? The all-seeing sun

speaker: ROMEO
text: Ne’er saw her match since first the world begun.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Tut, you saw her fair, none else being by,

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Herself poised with herself in either eye;

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: But in that crystal scales let there be weighed

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Your lady’s love against some other maid

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: That I will show you shining at this feast,

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: And she shall scant show well that now seems best.

speaker: ROMEO
text: I’ll go along, no such sight to be shown,

speaker: ROMEO
text: But to rejoice in splendor of mine own.
